name: gemstone-ide
description: GemStone/S IDE for Visual Studio Code
author: James Foster

gemstone.gciLibraries.description: Paths to GCI libraries, keyed by GemStone version (e.g. "3.7.4.3": "/path/to/libgcirpc-3.7.4.3-64.dylib")

gemstone.maxEnvironment.description: Maximum method dictionary environment to show in the browser (0 = standard Smalltalk only, 2 = show environments 0–2)

gemstone.sessionMode.description: Number of concurrent GemStone sessions allowed. 'single' (default) keeps one session at a time for a clearer mental model — the connected login shows an inline Logout action and the Sessions panel is hidden. 'multiple' (beta) re-enables concurrent sessions and the Sessions panel.

gemstone.exportPath.description: Root path for the local class mirror (read-only .gs files used by Find in Files / Go to Definition). Variables: {workspaceRoot}, {session}, {host}, {stone}, {user}, {index}, {dictName}. Defaults to {workspaceRoot}/.gemstone/{host}/{stone}/{user}/{index}-{dictName} — keyed by connection target so it is shared across that target's sessions and kept (and re-synced incrementally) across logout.

gemstone.classSync.readOnlyMirror.description: Write the local class-mirror .gs files as read-only, so they aren't edited directly (edit via the System Browser instead). Turn off to roughly halve the filesystem operations per class, which can noticeably speed up syncing on slow or network filesystems.

gemstone.rootPath.description: Root directory for GemStone installations and databases. Supports ~ for home directory.

gemstone.mcp.registerWithClaudeDesktop.description: Automatically register the gemstone MCP server with Claude Desktop. When enabled, Jasper writes a single user-scope `gemstone` entry into Claude Desktop's global `claude_desktop_config.json` on activation (macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/, Windows: %APPDATA%\Claude\, Linux: ~/.config/Claude/). The entry stays put across VS Code restarts. Claude Code registration writes to `~/.claude.json` and is always on.

gemstone.mcp.httpPort.description: Port on 127.0.0.1 where Jasper serves the MCP HTTPS/SSE surface. The URL to paste into connector UIs (e.g. Claude Desktop's 'Add custom connector') is https://127.0.0.1:<port>/sse. Jasper generates a self-signed TLS certificate on first run and stores it in the extension's global storage directory; trust it once via the 'GemStone: Install MCP TLS Certificate' command. First-come-first-served across VS Code windows — override this per-workspace in .vscode/settings.json to run multiple windows simultaneously.

sync_classes.description: Sync this connection's classes to the local .gemstone mirror (read-only .gs files for Find in Files / Go to Definition). Defaults to true; turn off for slow/remote connections.

gemstone.enhancedInspectorPerfTracking.description: Enable enhanced inspector perf tracking: count GCI round trips in the enhanced inspector. When enabled, shows a status bar counter and logs to the 'GemStone Enhanced Inspector Perf' output channel. Intended for performance tuning only.

enumDescriptions: When a stone without enhanced inspector support is connected, offer to install it.
Install enhanced inspector support automatically (requires the SystemUser default password; otherwise a notification explains how to install manually).
Never offer to install enhanced inspector support.
gemstone.enhancedInspector.autoInstall.description: What to do on connecting to a stone that does not have enhanced inspector support installed. Only applies to GemStone 3.7.5 and later, which the enhanced inspector requires; older stones are never offered it. The buttons on the install prompt update this setting.

enumDescriptions: Non-destructive: show the result as an inline annotation that is not part of the document, so the file is never modified. Hover the result for Copy and Expand actions.
Classic: insert the result into the document as editable text (a single Backspace removes it). This dirties the file.
gemstone.displayItMode.description: How 'Display It' (Ctrl+K D / Cmd+K D) shows its result.

gemstoneSmalltalk.formatter.spacesInside.description: Add spaces inside parentheses: ( x ) instead of (x)
Add spaces inside brackets: [ x ] instead of [x]
Add spaces inside braces: { x } instead of {x}

gemstoneSmalltalk.formatter.spacesAround.description: Add spaces around the assignment operator: x := y instead of x:=y
Add spaces around binary selectors: a + b instead of a+b

gemstoneSmalltalk.formatter.spaceAfterCa.description: Add space after return caret: ^ x instead of ^x

gemstoneSmalltalk.formatter.blankLineAft.description: Insert a blank line between the method pattern and body

gemstoneSmalltalk.formatter.maxLineLengt.description: Maximum line length for wrapping (0 = no wrapping)

gemstoneSmalltalk.formatter.continuation.description: Additional indentation for continuation lines (multi-keyword messages)

gemstoneSmalltalk.formatter.multiKeyword.description: Split keyword messages to multiple lines when keyword count reaches this threshold

gemstoneSmalltalk.formatter.removeUnnece.description: Remove unnecessary parentheses based on Smalltalk message precedence (unary > binary > keyword, left-to-right)

viewsWelcome.contents:
New to GemStone? Start with the guided walkthrough.
[Get Started with GemStone](command:gemstone.openWalkthrough)
Or learn the language in the interactive tutorial notebook.
[Open Tutorial Notebook](command:gemstone.openTutorial)
Or jump straight into a scratch workspace.
[Open Getting Started Workspace](command:gemstone.openWorkspace)
Then add a login to connect to a stone.
[Add a Login](command:gemstone.login)

No objects inspected yet.
Select code and press Cmd+K I to inspect.

No objects inspected yet.
Select code and press Ctrl+K I to inspect.

GemStone system administration requires Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL2).
[Install WSL](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install)

GemStone system administration requires Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL2).
[Install WSL](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install)

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with GemStone
walkthroughs.description: Connect to a stone and explore Smalltalk expressions in Jasper.

steps.title: Connect to a stone
steps.description:
Add a login in the Logins & Sessions view and click it to connect.
[Add a Login](command:gemstone.login)

steps.title: Open a workspace
steps.description:
Open a scratch buffer to try out Smalltalk expressions.
[Open Workspace](command:gemstone.openWorkspace)

steps.title: Run your first expression
steps.description:
Select an expression and Display It to evaluate it inline.
[Display It](command:gemstone.displayIt)

steps.title: Inspect an object
steps.description:
Inspect It opens the result in the Inspector to drill into its fields.
[Inspect It](command:gemstone.inspectIt)

steps.title: Try the enhanced inspector
steps.description:
Get richer, object-specific views in the Inspector. Requires GemStone 3.7.5 or later. Choose how Jasper installs it when you connect.
[Enhanced Inspector Auto-Install…](command:gemstone.configureEnhancedInspectorAutoInstall)

steps.title: Reopen this walkthrough later
steps.description:
Closed this? Reopen it anytime from Help → Welcome, or the Command Palette.
[Open Walkthrough...](command:welcome.showAllWalkthroughs)

commands.title: Reset Getting Started (Show Walkthrough on Next Connect)
Toggle Inline Values On Every Line
Show Display It Result in Output
Expand Display It Result In Place
Open Class Definition to the Side
Open Class Definition to the Side

view/item/context.when: view == gemstoneProcesses && viewItem == gemstoneProcessNetldi && gemstone.isWindows && gemstone.wslAvailable

scripts.compile: npm run compile:client && npm run compile:server && npm run compile:mcp
scripts.//test:gci: Runs only the on-demand 'gci' vitest project (client/vitest.config.ts); needs a live test stone (npm run test:server:start). `npm test` runs the 'default' project and excludes these.
scripts.test:gci: cd client && npx vitest run --project gci
scripts.//test:acceptance: End-user acceptance tests that drive a real VS Code window (Playwright + Electron). Slow and GUI-bound, so excluded from `npm test`. Run `npm run compile` first.
scripts.publish: npm run publish:vsce && npm run publish:ovsx
